name: Boards of Canada
bio: Boards of Canada earn through cult-level listener loyalty, deep album listening, and a catalog that fits ambient, downtempo, and nostalgic electronic use cases.
notable_for: Warm analog textures, cult following, and deep-catalog listening behavior.
career_highlight: Their music keeps circulating through long-form listening, reissues, and discovery among electronic music audiences.
revenue_drivers: Dedicated listeners drive steady long-tail streaming despite limited mainstream exposure.
Vinyl, reissues, and collector demand strengthen catalog economics.
Mood-based playlist use keeps key tracks active over time.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Boards of Canada's current headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Boards of Canada's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
